feature: Verify roof pump follows any machine pump (takpump)
summary: Confirms the existing roof-cooling logic implements your rule: if ANY machine valve opens the roof pump runs, and it stays on until ALL machine valves are off. The inputs are already wired to the OR gate; this playbook walks the chain so you can confirm the OUTPUT path (gate -> TAKSTATUS -> plant enable) is correct.

steps.title: Open the roof-cooling task group
steps.detail: In the Configurator tree, expand Tasks and select this group. It holds the two gates, the TAKSTATUS flag, and the two control Programs.
steps.verify: You see 'OR Ventiler På status', 'AND Ventiler På status', a TAKSTATUS boolean, and the Uppstart/Avstängning Programs.

steps.title: Check the OR gate is the 'any-on' trigger
steps.target: Tasks\Kylvatten anläggning tak 84000-CM1\OR Ventiler På status
steps.detail: Open this Gate. In the inspector (left pane), confirm Type = 1 (OR) and that it has 5 inputs, one per machine valve 'På status' signal.
steps.verify: Settings show Type=1; Inputs list the 5 Maskin 84001B/84002B/84003B/84004/84005 valve signals. OR = roof on when any one is on, off only when all are off.

steps.title: Confirm the AND gate is NOT the shut-off
steps.target: Tasks\Kylvatten anläggning tak 84000-CM1\AND Ventiler På status
steps.detail: Open this Gate (Type = 0 / AND, same 5 inputs). AND is only true when EVERY machine is on. Make sure your shut-off logic does NOT use this gate to turn the roof pump off - 'all off' must be handled by the OR gate going false, not the AND gate.
steps.verify: Type=0 (AND). If a Program uses this gate to STOP the pump, that is a bug; the OR gate going false is what should stop it.

steps.title: Trace the Program that sets TAKSTATUS
steps.detail: Open this Program. Confirm it READS the OR gate output and WRITES it to the TAKSTATUS flag (If OR gate = true -> SetValue TAKSTATUS = true; else false). This is the link the gate output itself doesn't carry, so the Program must do it.
steps.verify: The Program's If-condition reads 'OR Ventiler På status' and its SetValue target is TAKSTATUS. If it instead keys off the AND gate, that's the issue to fix.

steps.title: Confirm TAKSTATUS drives the plant enable
steps.target: Tasks\Kylvatten anläggning tak 84000-CM1\Avstängning/Start av Kylvatten anläggning
steps.detail: Open this Program. Confirm it maps TAKSTATUS to the LoRa-Modbus plant enable 'Devices\Lora-Modbus\Slave\84000 Spärra' in the correct direction (pump should RUN when TAKSTATUS true; Spärra = block, so check whether it sets Spärra=false to allow running, or sets a run command - get the polarity right).
steps.verify: Direction is correct: any machine on -> TAKSTATUS true -> plant allowed to run; all machines off -> TAKSTATUS false -> plant stopped/blocked.

steps.title: Live test with one machine
steps.detail: With the logic confirmed, in the live client open one machine valve only. The roof pump should start. Open a second machine - still running. Close the first - still running (second still on). Close the last - roof pump stops.
steps.verify: Roof pump runs while ANY machine valve is open and stops only when the LAST one closes. That is your intended takpump behavior.
